skill: Systems of two linear equations in two variables
stem: A concert venue sells two types of tickets, student tickets for $10 and general admission tickets for $15. If the venue sold 40 tickets for a total of $510, how much, in dollars, was spent on general admission tickets? (Disregard the $ sign when gridding your answer.)
rationale: The correct answer is 330. Let s be student tickets and g be general tickets. Then s + g = 40 and 10s + 15g = 510. Substituting s = 40 − g: 10(40 − g) + 15g = 510, so 400 + 5g = 510 and g = 22. Total spent on general admission tickets is 22 · 15 = 330.
